per_criterion.rationale: Axis B: openings carry specific entities (gofreddy audits, $80k deals, 'Most agencies audit digital maturity by counting tool SKUs') and concrete schema-violation framings; Axis A: forward-vector mostly bounded ('You ask which 6 workflows? They can't name 4'); Axis C: bodies generally deliver the gap, but several drafts open with topic-statement framing ('OpenAI's B2B Signals gives enterprises the language they needed') and the build-02 'Before: X / Now: Y' shape predicts itself from the title, weakening hook-body tension across the cohort.

evidence.quote: All claims stay within voice.md allowlist (gofreddy, autoresearch, x_engine)

per_criterion.rationale: Step 1: central claim is that 'AI adoption' is theater unless workflows are agentic across 6 named operational areas, and that agencies should sell against maturity tiers (1/6 to 4/6) rather than vague 'AI consulting'; Step 2: a thoughtful peer could substantively disagree — e.g., 'workflow agentic-ness is the wrong leverage point, ICP-fit matters more,' or 'most enterprises score 4-5/6 already, the framework overstates the gap,' or contest the pricing model ('$12k per workflow tier is unrealistic vs. retainer'); Step 3: claim is causal + prescriptive + falsifiable on substance, not stylistic.

evidence.quote:
Now you sell against maturity, not hype.

Here's what changes:

Before: "Your content process needs AI."
Now: "You're at 1/6 on content workflows. I'll get you to 4/6 in 12 weeks."
Turns out, most enterprises "use AI" in 2-3 workflows max. The other 9 are humans doing AI tasks.

per_criterion.rationale: Step 1: cohort spans sharp (256-278), build (648-728), case_study (1247) brackets; Step 2: SHARPs hold single coherent claims that resolve in-post; CASE_STUDY narrative (jr-2026-05-12-005-case-study.md, jr-2026-05-12-b2b-signals-case-study-01.md) reveals new beats per section; BUT build-02 'Before: X / Now: Y' triptych restates the same maturity-pricing point three times (positioning, pricing, framing) with no new revelation per unit, and build-01's diagnostic list and 'Cost structure flips' close are restatements of the opening; Step 3: structure mostly earns its bracket but multiple BUILD units fail Rate-of-Revelation.

per_criterion.rationale: Step 1: cold-start regime (no prior posts in source_data); positioning is 'AI-native marketing' / operator-efficiency; Step 2: drafts read as LinkedIn-shape imported to X — 'Here's the shift', 'The agency play:', 'The sales moment:', 'The close:', 'The move:' lesson-extracting closes are broadcast-mentor tone, not peer-in-the-conversation; Step 3: slop stack triggers across multiple drafts (→-arrow parallel listicles + reflexive tricolons + 'You're not selling X. You're selling Y.' Stop-X-Start-Y rhythm + 'Before/Now' antithesis parallelism + 'Cost structure flips' conclusive cadence) — ≥3 tells co-occurring; PLUS jargon-gloss violation: 'agentic workflows,' 'B2B Signals,' 'ICP,' 'GTM,' '0/6 agentic,' 'NRR' appear unglossed and break the accessible-to-non-engineer-founder/marketer contract.

evidence.quote: Cost structure flips. You're not selling "AI consulting." You're selling "from 2 to 6 agentic workflows in 12 weeks."
The agency play: **Be the person who says the quiet part out loud.**
At-risk customer signals existed in the data (declining NRR, support ticket volume, login frequency below cohort median).

per_criterion.rationale: Eight drafts for angle 121 collapse to one primary differentiator ('B2B Signals = agentic-workflow-audit framework agencies sell as maturity tiers'), one source (OpenAI B2B Signals research), and a narrow set of hook archetypes ('we ran an audit / founder said X / most enterprises score Y/6'); pillar diversity is single ('AI-native marketing' on 7 of 8) despite the angle metadata permitting 'operator-efficiency'; sharp-01 + sharp-02 are near-paraphrases of the same line ('B2B Signals gives enterprises the language' / '\"We use AI\" tells you nothing'); case-study-01 and 005-case-study.md both run the 'audit reveals 0-2/6 across content/sales/retention' narrative — swap-test: drafts could be substituted for one another with negligible variant value.

"We use AI" tells you nothing. "We use agentic workflows in 4 of 6 operational areas" tells you everything.
This founder scored 0/6. They licensed tools. They didn't automate workflows.
Score: 1/5 agentic. Theater.

evidence.quote: For anyone selling AI transformation to enterprises: you need this framework. Go read it. Changes how you diagnosis.

The move: run the same audit on your 3 biggest clients this month.

Evaluation restricted to angle-121 drafts (jr-2026-05-12-003-audit, jr-2026-05-12-004-vaporware, jr-2026-05-12-005-case-study, jr-2026-05-12-b2b-signals-{build-01,build-02,case-study-01,sharp-01,sharp-02}).
